_comment: Copy to ~/.claude/settings.json and customize for your machine

permissions._comment_permissions: MCP wildcards require server name: mcp__servername__* (not mcp__*). Add one entry per MCP server. For tighter control, replace broad tool names with granular rules like: Bash(git add:*), Read(/path/to/project/**)
permissions._comment_deny: Deny remote Claude.ai connectors that duplicate locally-configured MCP servers. Local servers (in ~/.claude/mcp.json) take precedence -- they use direct LAN connections with Bearer auth instead of OAuth through Cloudflare tunnels. Add entries here for any MCP server that has both a local and remote configuration.

hooks.prompt:
<autolearn>
NEVER block, reject, or stop user input. This is passive context only.
As you work: note mistakes you correct, gotchas you hit, and patterns you discover.
When a task completes and notable learnings were produced, run /autolearn autonomously.
Do not ask permission. Do not show a menu. Announce briefly: "Capturing learnings from this task."
Triggers: you corrected an error, hit a platform gotcha, made an architectural decision, or the session is ending after productive work.
The skill auto-selects the right workflow based on session context. For explicit control: /autolearn review, /autolearn audit, etc.
</autolearn>

<verification-reminder>
Before committing, pushing, or merging, verify:
1. Build passes (go build, tsc, dotnet build, cargo build)
2. Tests pass (go test, vitest, dotnet test, cargo test)
3. Lint passes (golangci-lint, eslint, cargo clippy)
Core Principle #2: Build, test, and lint must pass before any commit. No exceptions.
</verification-reminder>

_comment_plugins: Add language-specific LSP plugins as needed: gopls-lsp, typescript-lsp, rust-analyzer-lsp, csharp-lsp, pyright-lsp. Add service plugins as needed: sentry, Notion, playwright, greptile, ralph-loop, frontend-design
